title: Existing-report form state can carry across addresses when record keys collide
reasoning: The hydration key includes the normalized address only for empty reports. For existing reports it is just updatedAt plus version, so switching the mounted editor from address A to address B will not reset title/body if both records share the same version and updatedAt. That is plausible for imported/backfilled records or two freshly-created v1 reports with identical timestamp precision. The UI would then compare address A's retained draft against address B's data, mark it dirty, and a save would PUT the wrong body/title to address B.
reproduction: Mount AddressReportEditor with report A {version:1, updatedAt:'2026-05-07T00:00:00Z', body:'A'}, switch to edit and change body, then rerender the same component for address B whose fetched report is {version:1, updatedAt:'2026-05-07T00:00:00Z', body:'B'}. The textarea remains the A draft instead of hydrating to B.
recommendation: Include the normalized address, and preferably a content fingerprint, in the existing-report record key, for example `${normalizedAddress}:${data.updatedAt}:${data.version}:${data.title ?? ''}:${data.body}` or a small stable fingerprint. This matches the repo's documented pending-ledger guidance that content fingerprints belong in remount/hydration keys when updatedAt can be preserved.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The existing test only covers address changes where both reports are empty-state records. There is no test for changing address while both SWR results are existing reports with colliding updatedAt/version values.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a jsdom test that renders an existing report for VALID_ADDR, edits the textarea, rerenders the same mounted editor for SECOND_VALID_ADDR with mockSwrData changed to an existing report that has the same updatedAt/version but different body/title, and asserts the fields hydrate to the second report.
minimumFixScope: Update AddressReportEditor's recordKey construction and extend address-report-editor.test.tsx with the existing-report address-switch regression.

history.note: AddressReportEditor existing-report hydration keys include address plus content fingerprint, with same-version/updatedAt address-switch regression.
